name: TEJON RANCH CO
business: Tejon Ranch Co. is a diversified real estate development company that develops, leases, and sells commercial/industrial, multifamily, and resort/residential properties, and also generates revenue from mineral resources (oil/gas royalties, rock/aggregate, cement lease) and farming (almonds, pistachios, wine grapes, olives). The company operates on approximately 270,000 acres of contiguous land in California, anchored by the Tejon Ranch Commerce Center (TRCC), a 20 million-square-foot commercial and industrial development. Revenues come from leasing to tenants, land sales, joint ventures, and commodity sales.
sellsTo: Farm product buyers (almonds, pistachios, wine grapes, olives)
namedCustomers: National Cement Company of California, Inc.

exposures.sentence: Over eight million square feet of industrial, commercial, and retail space has been developed at TRCC, including properties owned and operated by us as well as facilities constructed by third parties on land previously sold by the Company. These developments are operational, or expected to commence construction in the near term, and include distribution centers for IKEA, Caterpillar, Nestlé, Famous Footwear, L'Oreal, Camping World, Sunrise Brands, Dollar General and RectorSeal.

The Company installed a solar-covered parking structure at the Outlets at Tejon. The structure covers 1.85 acres and is projected to reduce by approximately 83% the outlet center’s electricity consumption needs for shared spaces and produce approximately 1,076,000-kilowatt hour, or kWh, of clean energy every year.

The Company also installed solar-covered parking structures at Terra Vista at Tejon. The structure covers 0.25 acres and is projected to reduce by approximately 100% Terra Vista's electricity consumption needs for shared spaces and produce approximately 223,000-kilowatt hour, or kWh, of clean energy every year.
Combined, the west and east sides of TRCC contain nearly 100 superchargers. The facility is owned by the Company.
We lease certain portions of our land to oil companies for the exploration and production of oil and gas. We do not engage in any oil exploration or extraction activities.
Our farming operations produce revenues from the sale of wine grapes, almonds, and pistachios, and we are expanding our permanent crop portfolio to include olives.
The addition of multifamily residential leasing further diversifies our portfolio and is expected to enhance long-term recurring revenue streams.
Terra Vista at Tejon ... 228-unit multifamily community ... leasing commenced in May 2025.
We are a diversified real estate development company anchored by the Tejon Ranch Commerce Center (TRCC), a 20 million-square-foot commercial and industrial development.
Information technology failures and data security breaches could harm our business.

catalysts.event: Completion of first phase (228 units) of Terra Vista at Tejon multifamily development
catalysts.sentence: The first phase of Terra Vista at Tejon was completed in 2025 and represents the first phase of development, consisting of 228 units.

catalysts.event: Leasing commenced at Terra Vista at Tejon
Court of Appeal issued decision affirming rescission of Centennial approvals
catalysts.sentence: On June 26, 2025, the Court of Appeal issued a written decision affirming the Superior Court’s judgment.

catalysts.event: Superior Court issued writ of mandate to set aside Centennial approvals
catalysts.sentence: On September 22, 2025, the Superior Court issued a writ of mandate ordering Los Angeles County to set aside the Centennial approvals.

catalysts.event: LA County rescinded and set aside Centennial approvals
catalysts.sentence: On December 2, 2025, LA County rescinded and set aside the Centennial approvals.

catalysts.event: Filed updated shelf registration statement on Form S-3
catalysts.sentence: In May 2025, we filed an updated shelf registration statement on Form S-3 that went effective in May 2025.

catalysts.event: Began reporting Multifamily as separate operating segment
catalysts.sentence: In connection with the commencement of lease-up at Terra Vista at Tejon, we began reporting Multifamily as a separate operating segment in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Southern California industrial market is 2 billion square feet
tamClaims.sentence: The Los Angeles industrial market is the largest in the country by many measures, sitting at the center of a 2 billion square-foot Southern California industrial market.
